title: Deterministic SVG renderer (model + template → SVG)
context: The project is data-first: the durable asset is the formation model; PDFs are a projection. The renderer is the core engine that turns a validated GlyphModel + SheetTemplate + StyleVariant + PaperSize into a deterministic SVG. Determinism is critical: identical inputs must produce byte-stable SVG output so that reviewer diffs are meaningful and golden-file CI tests can catch regressions. The renderer must have no vendor logic and no network dependency — all assets are vendored.
objective: Implement packages/core: a pure, deterministic TypeScript renderer that takes (GlyphModel, SheetTemplate, StyleVariant, PaperSize) as inputs and produces a well-formed SVG, with golden-file tests proving byte-stability across runs.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core exports a render function: (GlyphModel, SheetTemplate, StyleVariant, PaperSize) → SVG string.
Output is deterministic: identical inputs produce byte-identical SVG across invocations and environments (no timestamps, no random IDs).
Renderer supports at minimum a 4-line/3-zone guideline system for Latin (ascender/x-height/baseline/descender); guideline geometry is parameterized per GuidelineSystem from the schema.
Golden-file SVG tests committed in CI; a test run that produces a different SVG fails the build.
No vendor-specific logic, no network calls at render time; all assets (font outlines) passed as parameters or vendored.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; TypeScript + ESM + pnpm workspace conventions; DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md section 6 (Key decisions — determinism, SVG as source of truth)
output: packages/core with a working deterministic SVG renderer, golden-file test fixtures, and passing CI — committed via PR with DCO sign-off.
